active_backlog.linkedin_final_copy:
Most MCP discovery signals still are not working endpoints.

Current Not Human Search digest: 4,173 agent-ready sites indexed. 2,257 publish llms.txt. 456 pass a live JSON-RPC MCP initialize handshake. That is 10.9%.

The distinction matters. A string match for MCP in llms.txt or a static manifest is a citation. A working MCP endpoint has to complete the call.

If you are building agents against static MCP directories, the failure mode is wasted connections and context on endpoints that never initialize.

Full methodology and source links:
https://8bitconcepts.com/research/q2-2026-mcp-ecosystem-health.html

Live digest:
https://nothumansearch.ai/digest.json
facts_verified: https://nothumansearch.ai/digest.json returned total_sites=4173, mcp_verified=456, pct_mcp=10.9, llms_txt_count=2257, pct_llms_txt=54.1, openapi_count=485, pct_openapi=11.6

https://nothumansearch.ai/mcp returned HTTP 200 and advertises tools including verify_mcp, search_agents, get_site_details, get_stats, submit_site, check_url, register_monitor, list_categories, get_top_sites, recent_additions, and find_mcp_servers
The paper states MCP verification means a live JSON-RPC initialize handshake, not a string match or static manifest
